codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of search filters and comprehensive quality tests. SearchFilters struct with AND semantics, custom glob matcher, post-retrieval apply_filters() utility, over-fetch strategy (3× + 20 minimum) to compensate for filtering, and proper wiring through both hybrid and BM25-fallback search paths. 55 new tests (37 quality + 15 unit + 3 CLI) covering all expected behaviors: symbol lookup accuracy (80%+ top-1, 90%+ top-3), NL intent search, cross-file discovery, result structure completeness, snippet fidelity, and filter combinations. Code is clean, well-documented, and follows existing conventions.

issues.description: Custom glob_match_recursive uses recursive backtracking that handles `**` only when it appears as a prefix (via strip_prefix("**/")). Pattern `src/**` (trailing double-star without slash) fails to match paths deeper than one level (e.g., `src/sub/foo.rs`) because the `**` falls through to the single `*` handler which doesn't cross `/`. Common patterns like `**/*.rs` and `src/**/*.rs` work correctly. This is a minor limitation for an uncommon pattern.
Over-fetch multiplier (3×) and minimum offset (+20) are hardcoded magic numbers. Could be extracted to VeraConfig for tunability, but acceptable for current scope.
SearchFilters does not derive Serialize/Deserialize. This may be needed when the MCP server feature needs to accept or expose filter parameters in JSON. Not blocking for current scope.

File is 570 lines (over 500-line hard limit). Commit message explains this is due to ~200 lines of inline test modules; production code is within budget. Acceptable per AGENTS.md convention (must explain in commit message, which was done).
File is 551 lines (over 500-line hard limit). Same rationale as types.rs — inline test module adds ~60 lines. Explained in commit message.

Test file is 1028 lines. While test files are not explicitly exempt from size budgets (unlike spikes/benchmarks), the comprehensive test coverage justifies the size. Each test is focused and self-contained.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that Python methods inside classes are chunked as part of the parent class chunk (not as separate method chunks). This means searching for a method like 'find_by_username' returns the UserRepository class chunk. This affects symbol lookup accuracy for class methods and is relevant to future chunking improvements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues[0]: 'Python methods inside classes are chunked as part of the parent class chunk... Top-1 accuracy for class methods is lower than for standalone functions.' Also visible in search_quality_tests.rs:symbol_lookup_find_by_username test which checks content match rather than symbol_name match.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker suggested documenting that post-retrieval filtering is the preferred approach for search filters (vs modifying BM25/vector search internals). This is an architectural pattern choice that keeps the core pipeline simple and filters composable. Could be documented in architecture.md.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The skill could mention that post-retrieval filtering is the preferred approach for search filters... since it keeps the core pipeline simple and filters are composable.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description's advisory notes about config lookup tasks and cross-file discovery weaknesses were not addressed with code changes (filename/filetype matching stage, graph-lite metadata). These remain as documented limitations. The advisory used soft language ('Consider', 'may help') suggesting they were not strict requirements.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description notes: 'config lookup tasks are hard for all tools... Consider adding a filename/filetype matching stage' and 'Cross-file discovery is also weak... graph-lite metadata signals may help here.' Neither implemented, both remain open improvement areas.

summary: PASS. The search-quality feature is well-implemented with comprehensive test coverage. SearchFilters provides clean post-retrieval filtering with --lang, --path glob, --type, and --limit. The custom glob matcher handles common patterns correctly (minor limitation with trailing ** without slash). Over-fetch strategy compensates for post-filter result reduction. All 37 quality tests verify the expected behaviors: 80%+ top-1 symbol accuracy, NL intent relevance in top-5, cross-file discovery from 2+ files, complete result structure, descending scores, and all filter types. No blocking issues found. The two 500+ line files are justified by inline test modules and explained in the commit message per project conventions.
